identity.title: CS Engineering Student
identity.subtitle: Full-stack developer | AI/ML enthusiast | Problem solver
identity.status: Open to internships and exciting projects

bio.shortBio: A CS student passionate about building interactive experiences and solving complex problems with code.
bio.longBio: I'm a Computer Science student with a deep passion for full-stack development and machine learning. I started my journey with fundamentals, built my way through web development, and now explore the intersection of AI and interactive systems. Every project is a stepping stone in understanding how technology shapes the future.
bio.philosophy: Code is not just about functionality; it's about creating experiences that matter.
bio.currentFocus: Building intelligent, interactive systems at the intersection of AI and web development
bio.futureAspiration: Create tools that empower people to solve real-world problems

phase1.title: Fundamentals Phase
phase1.description: Started with data structures and algorithms, learning the building blocks of computer science.
phase1.keyMoment: Realized that code is like composing music—every piece matters.

phase2.title: Intermediate Phase
phase2.description: Dove into web development, built my first full-stack application, learned databases and backend systems.
phase2.keyMoment: First deployed app live on the internet. The feeling of users interacting with something I built.

phase3.title: Advanced Phase
phase3.description: Exploring ML, system design, open source contributions, and building AI-powered systems.
phase3.keyMoment: Realized that the future is about combining multiple domains—code, AI, design, data.

projects.title: Platonis.AI
projects.subtitle: Campus placement preparation platform for engineering students
projects.shortDescription: A comprehensive AI-powered preparation suite featuring local RAG-based aptitude quizzes, real-time code evaluation, and adaptive mock interviews.
projects.longDescription: Platonis.AI is a secure, local-first placement preparation platform built to help CS students practice and analyze their readiness across core domains. It integrates local vector indexing, fine-tuned LLM execution, adaptive speech-to-text scoring, and custom machine learning predictors—all operating entirely on free/local resources.

description.challenge: Building a multi-modal assessment system that is secure, local-first, and costs zero in marginal inference fees.
description.solution: Integrated Groq/Cerebras APIs for high-speed LLaMA 3.1 inference, combined with local SentenceTransformers for embeddings and ChromaDB for vector retrieval.
description.approach: Built modular FastAPI routers, SQLAlchemy ORM mappings, Next.js page routers, and local Python pipelines for speech scoring and computer vision.

keyLearnings: RAG architecture design and chunking optimization
Designing fallbacks and resiliency for free-tier LLM endpoints
Integrating real-time speech and face analysis models in browser
Managing relational states across multiple async agents

projects.title: Noetis.AI
projects.subtitle: Cognitive Drift Intelligence Platform for education
projects.shortDescription: Traces student conceptual understanding over time to detect knowledge decay using dynamic Graph Neural Networks and Transformers.
projects.longDescription: Noetis.AI analyzes massive student interaction logs (trained on CC BY-NC 4.0 EdNet dataset) to map and forecast how mastery fluctuates. Instead of predicting binary next-question correctness, it evaluates cognitive drift across interconnected concepts and alerts instructors with SHAP explainability and What-If simulation.

description.challenge: Detecting multi-concept knowledge decay patterns from sparse and imbalanced log sequences.
description.solution: Combined SAINT-based Transformer embeddings, GraphSAGE GNN mastery propagation, and an XGBoost drift classifier with TreeSHAP feature attribution.
description.approach: Processed 131M+ EdNet rows, engineered rolling-window drift targets, built a custom PyTorch GNN, and served it via a FastAPI server connected to a React D3 dashboard.

keyLearnings: Processing large-scale time-series interaction logs (Pandas/Dask)
Formulating and training Graph Neural Networks on conceptual graphs
Interpreting tree ensemble models using SHAP attribution matrices
Creating interactive force-directed D3 visualizations for model insights

projects.title: Audita
projects.subtitle: Automated GitHub repository health and quality auditor
projects.shortDescription: Evaluates any public GitHub repository across 7 weighted categories to return a normalized score out of 100 with a letter grade.
projects.longDescription: Audita is a high-performance repository scanner that parses documentation, CI/CD pipeline configs, release cadence, and code quality elements via GitHub REST API v3. It features Caffeine-based memory caching, low latency, PDF report export, and a custom cold-start UI.

description.challenge: Fetching multiple repository pages from GitHub without hitting severe rate limits or causing long server wait times.
description.solution: Implemented parallel WebFlux WebClient requests coupled with an in-memory Caffeine cache (15min TTL, LRU eviction).
description.approach: Built a Spring Boot backend, a synchronous WebFlux ingestion pipeline, and an optimized single-page vanilla JS frontend for speed.

keyLearnings: Caching patterns and key eviction policies in Spring Boot
Writing reactive, non-blocking HTTP requests with WebClient
Creating lightweight frontends with pure HTML/CSS/JS and Marked.js
JVM container tuning for resource-constrained free hosting environments

projects.title: VoxGlobal AI
projects.subtitle: Event-driven cinematic dubbing & localization platform
projects.shortDescription: Coordinates decoupled video separation, translation, and forensic watermarking services using Redis Streams.
projects.longDescription: VoxGlobal AI is a cinematic-grade localization workflow monorepo. It features Next.js portals, a FastAPI API gateway, multiple custom Python audio/NLP pipeline workers, PostgreSQL with pgvector embeddings, and Redis Streams. Coordinated inside anArts Technologies internship.

description.challenge: Managing multi-stage audio processing tasks concurrently across multiple distributed microservices.
description.solution: Built an event-driven pub/sub architecture utilizing Redis Streams for message brokers and PostgreSQL pgvector for speaker profiles.
description.approach: Led architecture design, set up the Docker Compose monorepo, wrote API gateway routers, and optimized WavLM biometric verification.

keyLearnings: Microservice orchestration and monorepo management (PNPM)
Event-driven programming with Redis Streams
Database vectors (pgvector) and speaker identification
CI/CD automated release schedules

projects.title: VANGUARD-HFT v2
projects.subtitle: Real-time high-frequency trading manipulation detection system
projects.shortDescription: Processes high-throughput Kafka streams to detect market anomalies using Graph Neural Networks and BiLSTMs.
projects.longDescription: VANGUARD-HFT is an AI market surveillance tool. It monitors L2 order books in real-time, processes incoming events via Kafka, executes heuristic and deep learning models (GraphSAGE + BiLSTM), and alerts operators with full SHAP transparency.

description.challenge: Detecting complex patterns like spoofing and layering within sub-millisecond market events.
description.solution: Engineered a dual-stage filter: heuristics drop safe events, while PyTorch GNN/BiLSTM models inspect suspicious nodes.
description.approach: Contributed the React injector UI, fixed and optimized the ML model boundaries, and resolved logic in the detection engine.

keyLearnings: Apache Kafka topic partitioning and event consumption
Model compilation for low-latency deep learning inference
Interpreting real-time sequence attention outputs
Handling race conditions in multi-account coordination detection

projects.title: AlgoScale
projects.subtitle: AI-powered algorithmic complexity and cost analyzer
projects.shortDescription: Analyzes Python source code using Abstract Syntax Trees and Scikit-Learn to predict Big-O runtime and execution metrics.
projects.longDescription: AlgoScale extracts structural features from code snippets using Abstract Syntax Trees (AST) and uses trained RandomForest/GradientBoosting estimators to categorize code into 15 complexity classes and estimate runtime performance.

description.challenge: Mapping arbitrary source code text to numerical complexity metrics without execution sandbox risks.
description.solution: Constructed an AST parser to extract 18 code signature features, feeding into classification and regression ensembles.
description.approach: Built AST analysis pipelines, trained ensembles on 150k generated code rows, and created a Monaco-based React UI.

keyLearnings: Abstract Syntax Tree traversal and node inspection (Python `ast`)
Synthesizing large, balanced datasets for ML classifier training
Integrating Monaco Editor with custom token rules in React

projects.title: NexusERP
projects.subtitle: Mini project management and ERP system
projects.shortDescription: A MERN stack project management application featuring Kanban boards, team management, task comments, and Socket.io notifications.
projects.longDescription: NexusERP is a comprehensive business workflow platform. It separates control across Admin, Manager, and Developer roles, enabling granular tracking of task lifecycle, comments, time-logging, and real-time alerts.

description.challenge: Designing a synchronized task workflow with real-time updates and strict role validation.
description.solution: Utilized JWT role authorization middleware on Node.js/Express and integrated Socket.io rooms for instant change notifications.
description.approach: Created database structures with Mongoose, set up state management context, and drew Kanban board components.

keyLearnings: Role-based access control (RBAC) modeling in Express
Handling real-time pub/sub notifications over socket boundaries
Structuring database indices for low-latency project lookups

projects.title: The Devil's Advocate
projects.subtitle: Autonomous multi-agent AI debate consensus system
projects.shortDescription: Features three autonomous Gemini-powered agents debating user prompts to eliminate LLM hallucinations.
projects.longDescription: The Devil's Advocate implements a multi-agent debate design pattern. Agent A (The Visionary) proposes solutions, Agent B (The Inquisitor) finds logical cracks and critique, and Agent C (The Arbiter) resolves disputes to yield fact-checked final verdicts.

description.challenge: Guiding LLM agents through structured multi-turn debates without collapsing into infinite loops or agreement bias.
description.solution: Implemented strict API message parsing, dynamic prompt injection, and sequential state machines.
description.approach: Constructed a Flask server wrapping the Google GenAI SDK and connected a React UI with custom debate animations.

keyLearnings: Multi-agent architecture design and context history management
Handling rate limits and exceptions from LLM providers
Animating state changes for asynchronous processes in React

projects.title: Logo Turtle Compiler
projects.subtitle: Tokenizer, parser, and SVG generator for Logo programming language
projects.shortDescription: Translates Logo turtle commands into vector graphics using a custom compilation pipeline in Python.
projects.longDescription: Logo Turtle Compiler implements the standard phases of compilation (lexing, parsing, interpreting, and code generation) to turn Logo commands (like repeat, forward, right) into visual drawings.

description.challenge: Designing a parser that supports nested loops and translates them into an AST.
description.solution: Built a custom recursive descent parser that constructs nested program nodes and an interpreter that updates a vector canvas.
description.approach: Coded standard lexical rules, custom parser tokens, a turtle state interpreter, and a Flask server.

keyLearnings: Grammar rules modeling and parsing loops
Transforming 2D coordinates and angles to scalable SVG markup
Building simple compiler interfaces

projects.title: Reality Layer Portfolio
projects.subtitle: AI-native futuristic portfolio with particle systems
projects.shortDescription: An interactive, data-driven portfolio that reorganizes based on visitor intent using Canvas, D3.js, and AI.
projects.longDescription: This portfolio website represents the reality layer itself. It maps out skills, credentials, and projects using dynamic 3D elements, canvas particle physics, soundscapes, and CLI-to-GUI toggle states.

description.challenge: Creating an immersive, high-performance interface that seamlessly blends Terminal-like controls with 3D visualizations.
description.solution: Built React custom components, coupled with Three.js/Fiber for wireframe holograms, and a D3 force simulation.
description.approach: Constructed the theme variables, canvas render loops, audio manager nodes, and custom React hooks.

keyLearnings: WebGL canvas initialization and optimization in React
Synchronizing synthesized audio context to cursor events
Building state engines for command suggestion dropdowns

cliQuotes: Have you tried turning the CLI off and on again?
GUI is just CLI with extra steps.
I don't always test my code, but when I do, I do it in production.
In a world full of GUIs, be a CLI.
The command line is mightier than the mouse.
